rationale: The cited paper is used in the conceptual development as part of the key theoretical basis for the surprise mechanism, not just as background. The citing authors repeatedly invoke Heilman et al. (2002) to support the claim that unexpected promotional events trigger surprise, which is central to their sequential-presentation predictions and process tests. Although it is cited in a cluster with other surprise studies and not reused methodologically, it meaningfully informs framing and interpretation of core hypotheses.
relation_description: The citing paper relies on Heilman et al. (2002) as prior evidence that unexpected promotions induce surprise, then builds its sequential-percentage theory on that premise. This prior surprise evidence helps justify why the second percentage can capture attention and serve as the judgment anchor.
